metadata.summary: Fetch clients for the current tenant
metadata.description: Returns paginated client records. Use this before creating tickets to map human-readable client names to client_id values.

parameters.name: client_name
parameters.description: Optional case-insensitive filter on the client name.

parameters.name: is_inactive
parameters.description: When true, include inactive clients as well as active ones.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of records per page (default 25, max 100).

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Pagination page number starting at 1.

examples.name: Find a specific client
examples.notes: Use a small limit when searching for a client name mentioned by the user so you can quickly grab the matching client_id. If the user did not name a specific client, reuse the first client_id returned.

metadata.description: Paginated list of contacts. Filter by client_id to retrieve contacts for a specific client when preparing a ticket.

parameters.name: client_id
parameters.description: Restrict contacts to a specific client.

parameters.name: search_term
parameters.description: Search text to match against contact names or emails.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of records per page (default 25, max 100).

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Pagination page number.

examples.name: Find client contacts
examples.notes: Use the client_id discovered from the clients endpoint to retrieve matching contacts and resolve contact_name_id.

metadata.description: Returns the saved locations for the specified client. Call this when a ticket needs a location_id.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Client identifier whose locations should be listed.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of locations to return (default 25, max 100).

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Pagination page number.

examples.name: List locations for client
examples.notes: Use the same client_id supplied to the ticket create payload.

metadata.description: Returns paginated board records for the current tenant. Use this to discover valid board_id values before creating tickets or statuses. Each board has its own set of statuses, categories, and priorities.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Case-insensitive search on board name or description.

parameters.name: include_inactive
parameters.description: When true, include inactive boards (excluded by default).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of records per page (default 25, max 100).

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Pagination page number starting at 1.

examples.name: List active boards
examples.notes: Use the board_id from the response when creating tickets or querying statuses. The first active board is a safe default if the user does not specify a board.

metadata.description: Returns a single board record by its UUID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Board identifier.

metadata.description: Creates a new board. After creating a board, create at least one status for it via POST /api/v1/statuses so tickets can be assigned to the board.

is_default.description: Whether this is the default board.

examples.name: Create a new board
examples.notes: After creating, add statuses via POST /api/v1/statuses with the new board_id and status_type 'ticket'.

metadata.description: Updates an existing board. All fields are optional — only send the fields you want to change.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Board identifier.

metadata.description: Returns paginated status records. For ticket statuses, always filter by board_id to get statuses that belong to a specific board. Use this to resolve a valid status_id before creating or updating tickets.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Filter by status type: ticket, project, project_task, or interaction.

parameters.name: board_id
parameters.description: Required when type=ticket. Filter statuses belonging to a specific board.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Case-insensitive search on status name.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of records per page (default 25, max 100).

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Pagination page number starting at 1.

examples.name: List ticket statuses for a board
examples.notes: Always pair type=ticket with board_id. Use the status_id from results when creating tickets — the status_id must belong to the same board_id used on the ticket.

metadata.description: Creates a new status. For ticket statuses, board_id is required. The status_type must be one of: ticket, project, project_task, interaction.

status_type.description: Type of entity this status applies to.

board_id.description: Required for ticket statuses. The board this status belongs to.

is_closed.description: Whether this status represents a closed state.

is_default.description: Whether this is the default status for the board.

examples.name: Create a ticket status for a board
examples.notes: Use GET /api/v1/boards first to resolve the board_id. The board_id is required for ticket statuses.

metadata.description: Updates an existing status. All fields are optional — only send the fields you want to change. Cannot change status_type or board_id after creation.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Status identifier.

metadata.description: Provides the category catalog used when assigning category_id or subcategory_id to a ticket.

parameters.name: board_id
parameters.description: Filter categories that belong to a specific ticket board.

parameters.name: is_active
parameters.description: Toggle to include inactive categories.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of categories per page (default 25, max 100).

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Pagination page number.

examples.name: Categories for a board
examples.notes: Pair this with the board_id you plan to use on the ticket so category selections stay consistent.

metadata.description: Lists tenant users. Use this to discover the user_id for the assignee field on a ticket.

parameters.name: search_term
parameters.description: Match against name or email.

parameters.name: team_id
parameters.description: Filter users belonging to a specific team.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of users per page (default 25, max 100).

examples.name: Find a technician
examples.notes: Use the result set to map the human name back to a user_id for the assigned_to field.

metadata.description: Returns tags configured for the tenant. Reuse these when populating the tags array for a ticket.

parameters.name: entity_type
parameters.description: Limit results to tags used for a specific entity type (e.g., ticket).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of tags per page (default 25, max 200).

examples.name: Retrieve ticket tags
examples.notes: Use existing tag names whenever possible rather than inventing new ones.
